# terrible_azure_azcollection_azure_rm_containerregistrywebhook (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `registry_name` (String) The name of the container registry.
- `resource_group` (String) The name of the resource group to which the container registry belongs.
- `webhook_name` (String) The name of the webhook.

### Optional

- `actions` (String) The list of actions that trigger the webhook to post notifications.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `custom_headers` (String) Custom headers that will be added to the webhook notifications.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `location` (String) Resource location. If not set, location from the resource group will be used as default.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `scope` (String) The scope of repositories where the event can be triggered. For example, 'foo:*' means events for all tags under repository 'foo'. 'foo:bar' m eans events for 'foo:bar' only. 'foo' is equivalent to 'foo:latest'. Empty means all events.
- `service_uri` (String) The service URI for the webhook to post notifications.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Assert the state of the Container registery replication. Use `present` to create or update Container registery replication and `absent` to delete it.
- `status` (String) The status of the webhook at the time the operation was called.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
